title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for OSS permission  changes

input_row.title: Ensure log monitoring and alerts are set up for OSS permission  changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for OSS Bucket RAM changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring changes to OSS permissions may reduce time to detect and correct permissions on sensitive OSS bucket and objects inside the bucket.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to ensure log monitoring and alerts are set for OSS permission changes. 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page. 3. Ensure the OSS are Enabled under the Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page, and click Central Project. 4. Select Alerts. 5. Ensure below alert rule has been enabled and saved in the target oss_log (operation: PutBucket and request_uri: acl) or operation: PutObjectAcl| select bucket, count (1) as c group by bucket
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to ensure the log monitoring and alerts are set up for OSS permission changes: 1. Logon to SLS Console. 2. Click Log Service Audit Service in the navigation pane. 3. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration page. a. Select a location of project for logs. b. Check the OSS and configure a proper days. c. Click Save to save the changes. 4. Go to Access to Cloud Products > Global Configurations click Central Project. 80 | P a g e 5. Select Log Management > OSS Log. 6. In the search/analytics console, input below query (operation: PutBucket and request_uri: acl) or operation: PutObjectAcl| select bucket, count (1) as c group by bucket 7. Create a dashboard and set alert for the query result.
input_row.default_value: The monitoring dashboard and alert is not set by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log on to the Alibaba Cloud Console and navigate to the **SLS (Log Service)** dashboard.
2. Select **Log Service Audit Service** in the navigation pane to go to the Log Service Audit Service page.
3. Verify that **OSS** logging is enabled by clicking on **Access to Cloud Products > Global Configuration**. 
   - Ensure that the logs for the OSS are configured properly, including the location of project for logs.
4. Click on the **Central Project** option and then select **Log Management > OSS Log**.
5. In the analytics console, run the query: 
   - `select bucket, count(1) as c from oss_log where operation = 'PutBucket' and request_uri = 'acl' or operation = 'PutObjectAcl' group by bucket`
6. Verify that there is an alert rule set up corresponding to the query. 
   - The expected alert rule should trigger notifications whenever the specified conditions are met.
7. Confirm that a dashboard has been created that visualizes the logs for OSS permission changes and that alerts are configured to monitor this dashboard.

**Expected secure configuration:** The alert rule should be enabled and actively monitoring changes to OSS bucket permissions, and the logs should show recent activity relating to ACL changes.

**How to confirm compliance:** Check the alert settings and the log activity for the relevant operations to ensure they match the required criteria. If the configurations are in place and functioning as intended, the compliance will be confirmed.
